name: Zesty.io Composable CMS API
what_it_does: Composable headless CMS platform with REST and GraphQL APIs — enabling enterprise marketing teams to manage content with an intuitive structured content model, deliver via CDN-powered Content Delivery API, and extend with modular composable architecture including AI-powered content generation, media management, and multi-site management.
use_cases: Delivering headless content via Zesty.io Content Delivery API for Next.js or Gatsby frontends
Managing Zesty.io content models and instances via REST API for content migration automation
Triggering Zesty.io webhook on content publish for static site rebuild in CI/CD pipeline
Querying Zesty.io media library assets via API for digital asset management integration
Managing multi-site content via Zesty.io API for enterprise multi-brand content operations
not_for: Small personal websites (enterprise pricing targets larger teams)
Developers who need deep PHP/database access (SaaS-only, no self-hosting)
Simple blogs without structured content needs (use Ghost or WordPress)
best_when: A mid-market or enterprise marketing team needs a composable headless CMS with AI content tools, multi-site management, and CDN-delivered content — with a marketing-friendly editor alongside developer APIs.
avoid_when: You're a solo developer or small team where enterprise pricing isn't justified, or need self-hosted deployment.

auth.notes: SITEREF + token authentication for management API. App Access Token for read-only content delivery. OAuth2 for Zesty.io integrations and third-party apps. Bearer token auth. Webhook on content publish/update. JavaScript Node SDK (zesty-org/node-sdk). Role-based access control (admin, developer, publisher, contributor). Two separate tokens: management API token vs delivery token.

pricing.notes: San Diego company. Founded 2010 by Schuyler Vargas and Andy Fleming. Raised $38M Series B (2022) from Headline, Hearst Ventures. 1,000+ customers globally. Enterprise-focused headless CMS. AI content generation tools (Zesty.io AI). Multi-site management for brands with multiple properties. CDN-native content delivery. Native Next.js and React integration. Paragon for iPaaS integrations. Strong in media, retail, and enterprise marketing. Competing with Contentful and Contentstack at enterprise level.

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly documented; varies by plan

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST + GraphQL APIs. Two auth systems (management API vs delivery API). Webhook on publish. SOC2 and GDPR compliant. Annual contract. JavaScript SDK available. Rate limits not publicly documented. Enterprise pricing ($500/month+) high barrier. Multi-site management requires understanding of Zesty.io's SITEREF concept.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Content reads fully idempotent by SITEREF + item ZUUID. Media reads idempotent by ID. Content creation uses ZUUID for idempotency. Webhook delivery idempotent for same event.
known_agent_gotchas: Two separate auth tokens: management API token vs content delivery token (SITEREF + APP access token)
Zesty.io uses ZUUID for content item IDs — non-standard UUID format
Annual contract required — monthly billing not available on most plans
Multi-site requires separate SITEREF per site — agents must manage multiple SITEREFs
Rate limits not documented — contact sales for enterprise rate limits

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2 and GDPR compliant. Two auth systems (management vs delivery) — good separation of concerns. Role-based access control (admin, developer, publisher, contributor). OAuth2 for integrations. Content delivery tokens read-only — safe for client-side use. Management API tokens must be server-side only. US and EU data residency options.
